description: Aphanie (Aphanizomenon sp) is periodically observed in the surface water of the Baltic Sea.
Denitrix (Sulfurimonas gotlandica str. GD1) an Epsilonproteobacterium  was isolated in May 2005 from the Gotland Deep oxic-anoxic interface (215 m water depth).
Sulfato (Desulforhopalus MAG bin145) turned up in the redox transition zone (80 m water depth) at Gotland Deep in August 2019.
Methazir (Methanosarcina baltica GS1-AT) was recovered from sediment at a depth of 241 m in the Gotland Deep of the Baltic Sea in April 1993.
Voltothrix (Electrothrix aarhusiensis) was identified in 2017 from Baltic Sea sediments.
Chlorobi (Chlorobium phylotype BS-1) proved physiologically active at depths up to 80 m in May-June 2007 in the Black Sea water column.
Sulfurimonas phylotype (BS139) was documented in March 2005 at 192 m water depth in the mixing zone where the Bosporus Plume enters the Black Sea.
Sulfatin (Desulfonema Desulfosarcina) was uncovered with molecular tools in the anoxic water column of the Black Sea in December 2001.
Methano (Methanococcales MC1109) appeared in Black Sea sediments in April-May 2003.
Ferrious (Shewanella putrefaciens MR9) was observed at water depths of 80-90 m (Mn reduction) in the summer of 1988.
Tricky (Trichodesmium spp.) was visualized by epifluorescence microscopy within the warmer waters of the California Coast in the summer of 2008.
Nitroman (Nitrospina) was recorded at 100?200 m water depth in July and August of 2008 in the southern Gulf of California.
Globulus (SUP05 Thioglobus) was present in the oxygen minimum zone of the Gulf of California in July–August 2008.
Methanor (Methanococcoides MG1) was discovered in the sediment of the Eastern Tropical North Pacific oxygen minimum zone in January 2012.
Scalindrax (Scalindua profunda) turned out to inhabit the anoxic water layers of the ETNP in June 2013.
Lumini (Luminiphilus MAG2202) was collected from the DCM of the Northeast Indian Ocean at 5 m depth in April-June 2020.
Globula (SUP05 Thioglobus) was identified in the oxygen-minimum zone of the Bay of Bengal in January 2020.
Desulfas (uncharacterized sulfate-reducing bacteria) was detected in the Bay of Bengal oxygen-minimum zones in December 2015.
Methyli (Methylobacterium MAG1149) was retrieved from the Northeast Indian Ocean at 2000 m depth in April-June 2020.
Tricho (Trichodesmium sp.) was observed at 2 m depth in the water column of the Eastern Indian Ocean in March 2019.
Chlororex (Mesodinium rubrum) appeared in the surface waters of Cariaco Basin in April 2008.
Glubby (Thioglobus CB81 CarAnox_MAG_81) was documented in the anoxic water layer (143-900 m) of Cariaco Basin in May-November 2014.
Capsanox (Desulfocapsa CarAnox_MAG_361) was recovered from the anoxic water layer (900 m) of Cariaco Basin in May-November 2014.
Vibri (Vibrio alginolyticus 46 B7 TP 215m) was found at 215 m water depth in Cariaco Basin in May 2006.
Ziny (Scalindua sp.) was recorded in the low-oxygen water depths of Cariaco Basin in 2010?2011.
Crocos (Crocosphaera sp.) was present in the oxic surface water of the Peruvian OMZ in January 2009.
Noxie (Scalindua praevalens) turned up at 35 m in the oxygen-deficient zone of the Peruvian Coast in April 2015.
Desulfobul (Desulfobacterium autotrophicum HRM2) was identified at 60-80 m in the oxygen-minimum zone of the Peruvian Coast in December 2008-January 2009.
Rhodobi (Rhodobacterales HTCC2255) was observed at 5-20 m water depth off the Peruvian Coast in December 2008-January 2009.
Magneti (Magnetospirillum gryphiswaldense) was documented at 5 m water depth off the Peruvian Coast in December 2008-January 2009.
PseudoN (Pseudo-nitzschia) was detected in surface waters (0-2 m) of the Namibian Coast during an algal bloom in 2004.
Denitro (Arcobacter nitrogilis) was recovered in March 2004 from the water column (80 m depth) of the Namibian OMZ.
Desulfobis (Desulfobulbus sp) appeared in July 2018 in the sediment of the Namibian OMZ.
Sarox (SAR11 clade) was recorded in July 2018 in the surface water of the Namibian OMZ.
Margarita (Thiomargarita namibiensis) was discovered in sediments at 100 m water depth in April 1997 along the Namibian Coast.
Thalassa (Thalassiosira) was observed in the surface water of Saanich Inlet in 1974-1975.
Spinanix (Nitrospina sp) was identified at 10-200 m depth in Saanich Inlet in April 2010.
Baculor (Desulfobacula I034 bin137) turned up at 200 m water depth in Saanich Inlet in March 2010.
Sarix (SAR11) was found at 10 m in Saanich Inlet in 2015.
Ector (Ectothiorhodospiraceae) was documented at 100-200 m in Saanich Inlet in 2015.
Ceros (Chaetoceros) was present in the surface waters of the Arabian Sea in 1995-1997.
Pumilus (Nitrosopumilus) was detected in the hypoxic zone of the Arabian Sea oxygen-minimum zone in May 2022.
Marino (Marinobacter sp. H054 MH256066 T1) was recovered from the water column of the Arabian Sea in April-May 2015.
Monas (Alteromonas) was observed in the euphotic and bathypelagic water layers of the Arabian Sea in May 2022.
Noctilux (Noctiluca scintillans) has been detected in the water column of the Arabian Sea since the early 2000s between January and March.
Prochorus (Prochlorococcus sp) was identified in the oxygen-depleted water layer off the Chilean Coast in February 2018.
Puff05 (SUP05 Thioglobus) was found in the Chilean Coast oxygen-minimum zone between 70-200 m in January 2010.
Venty (Venteria ishoeyi) was discovered in Chilean Coast sediment samples collected at 35 m depth in December 2008.
Methanil (Methanohalophilus sp) was documented at 30-90 m depth in the water column off the Chilean Coast in April-May 2003 almost exclusively during active upwelling periods.
Spinanox (Nitrospina sp) turned up at 30-80 m in the water column off the Chilean Coast in 2010-2012.
Cyanorion is the first oxygenic phototrophic bacteria that is thought to have appeared about 2.08 billion years ago.
Denitrion is the first denitrifying bacteria that is believed to have emerged about 2.25 billion years ago.
Metharion is the first methanogenic archaea that is suspected to have originated around 3.77 billion years ago.
Sulfarion is the first sulfate-reducing bacteria that thought to have arisen around 1.91-2.42 billion years ago.
Luca is the ancestor to all organisms living on Earth that likely emerged around 4.52-4.35 billion years ago.
